metadata.summary: Break research into 3-5 sub-queries via Perplexity API with search_recency_filter, collect citations, de-duplicate sources, consolidate into markdown synthesis.

content.title: Perplexity AI for Research
content.summary: Decompose a research question into 3-5 targeted Perplexity sub-queries, dispatch each via the Perplexity REST API with explicit search_recency_filter, collect cited results, de-duplicate sources, and consolidate into a markdown synthesis with a source registry. Never query the full question at once — narrow sub-queries produce better coverage and citation quality.

why.p: Perplexity returns citations inline alongside synthesized answers, saving a separate synthesis step needed when using general-purpose LLMs. With 780 million search queries/month (May 2025) and 45 million active users, the API is production-ready. The key gotcha is that Pro Search (web UI) does not exist in the API; use sonar-large with search_recency_filter as the equivalent.

list.item: Fast, cited multi-source synthesis where manual source collection would take hours
Market sizing queries needing recent (under 6 months) data with traceable URLs
Competitive landscape sweeps producing a first-pass list of players and funding events
Trend research where breadth matters more than depth in the first pass
Validating or cross-checking a specific claim with multiple independent sources
Deep primary-source research — Perplexity aggregates web content, not proprietary data
When citations must be peer-reviewed or from specific databases (PubMed, SSRN, SEC EDGAR)
Real-time streaming data (stock prices, live social signals) — Perplexity crawls on delay
Tasks where context must persist across multiple sessions — Perplexity has no persistent memory
When the research question is too ambiguous — vague queries produce authoritative-sounding but unreliable results

section.name: query-strategy
section.p: Sub-query decomposition rules, recency filter usage, and source validation requirements for Perplexity-based research pipelines.

rule.statement: Decompose research questions into 3-5 narrow sub-queries, each targeting one specific sub-question (market size, key players, recent funding, etc.) rather than submitting the full question at once.
rule.rationale: Long-form synthesis across 10+ topics in a single query degrades quality. Narrow sub-queries produce higher citation density and more accurate answers per topic. Break, then merge results.

rule.statement: Set search_recency_filter explicitly on every API call. Never rely on the default "any" setting for fast-moving markets.
rule.rationale: The default "any" recency filter pulls stale content. For market sizing and competitive research, use "month" or "year" to avoid anchoring on outdated data. Available values: hour, day, week, month, year.

example.code: <perplexity_research> <main_question>What is the TAM for AI-native research tools for knowledge workers in 2026?</main_question> <sub_queries> <query>Global knowledge worker software market size 2025 2026 sources</query> <query>AI research tool vendors market share funding 2025</query> <query>Perplexity Elicit Consensus user base revenue estimates 2026</query> </sub_queries> <recency>month</recency> <output>Markdown with inline citations. Flag any claim with a single source.</output> </perplexity_research>
example.note: Three focused sub-queries, each targeting one dimension. Recency set explicitly. Single-source claims flagged automatically.

rule.statement: Record citations alongside each answer and validate at least 2 citations per critical claim by following the URL. Never pass Perplexity output downstream without the source list.
rule.rationale: Perplexity links decay within days for news sources. Citations must be verified at time of research, not at time of report delivery. The citation list is the audit trail.

rule.statement: Use sonar-huge only for final synthesis steps; use sonar-small or sonar-large for exploratory queries.
rule.rationale: Cost scales with model size. Exploratory queries do not require frontier-level reasoning. Reserve sonar-huge for the final consolidation pass where quality matters most.

antipattern.description: Submitting the full research question as a single Perplexity query.
antipattern.reason: Broad single queries produce shallow coverage. The model synthesizes from fewer sources and produces lower citation density than decomposed sub-queries.

antipattern.description: Pro Search has a query-per-day cap on the API tier; high-volume agent loops hit the cap within hours.
antipattern.reason: Budget daily query counts per pipeline stage. Add exponential backoff and a fallback to web-search when the cap is hit.

section.name: agentic-patterns
section.p: API dispatcher patterns, parallel sub-query execution, CLI reference, and agent gotchas for Perplexity-driven pipelines.

rule.statement: A Claude Sonnet orchestrator decomposes the research question, dispatches each sub-query to the Perplexity REST API independently, then merges results into a consolidated synthesis with a source registry. Add 1-2 second delays between parallel sub-queries to respect QPM rate limits.
rule.rationale: Query-per-minute rate limits are stricter than daily caps. Parallel calls without delays will be rejected silently, producing incomplete results that look complete.

section.p: CLI and SaaS tools for Perplexity research:

list.item: requests — Call Perplexity REST API (no install needed)
anthropic SDK — Orchestrate query decomposition and synthesis (pip install anthropic)
httpx — Async Perplexity calls for parallel sub-queries (pip install httpx)
Perplexity API — SaaS, REST; models: sonar-small, sonar-large, sonar-huge; return_citations=true
Perplexity Spaces — SaaS, web-only; no API access; do not rely on in agent pipelines
Perplexity Pro Search (UI) — use sonar-large model in API as equivalent; no direct API toggle

antipattern.description: The Perplexity API does not expose Spaces; any Space-based context must be manually copied into the agent's prompt.
antipattern.reason: Agents that assume Spaces are API-accessible will silently skip that context, producing results that contradict the project's existing research.

antipattern.description: return_citations in the API returns URL strings, not structured metadata; the agent must parse source domains to assess credibility.
antipattern.reason: Without domain parsing, the agent cannot distinguish authoritative sources (Gartner, Statista, government data) from low-authority sources (SEO blogs, forums).

antipattern.description: Models available via API change without notice; pinning the model name is required with a fallback.
antipattern.reason: Unpinned model calls may silently switch to a different capability tier after a Perplexity API update, changing output quality and cost without warning.

antipattern.description: Perplexity will confidently synthesize from low-authority sources (forums, SEO blogs) alongside reputable ones; source filtering is the agent's responsibility.
antipattern.reason: The API does not rank sources by authority. The orchestrating agent must filter or weight by domain before presenting results as research findings.

template.purpose: Sync and async Perplexity API dispatchers
XML prompt template for query decomposition

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Best practices for Perplexity-based research pipelines:

list.item: Decompose research questions into 3-5 narrow sub-queries; broad single queries produce shallow coverage
Set search_recency_filter explicitly — default "any" pulls stale content for fast-moving markets
Record citations alongside each answer; never pass Perplexity output downstream without the source list
Validate at least 2 citations per critical claim by following the URL — Perplexity links decay within days for news sources
Use sonar-huge only for final synthesis steps; sonar-small or sonar-large for exploratory queries to control cost
Export Spaces (web) for long-running projects where human researchers also contribute — but do not rely on Spaces in agent pipelines

section.name: ai-agent-considerations
section.p: Critical considerations when building AI agents on Perplexity:

list.item: The API does not expose Perplexity Spaces; any Space-based context must be manually copied into the agent's prompt
return_citations in the API returns URL strings, not structured metadata; the agent must parse source domains to assess credibility
Query-per-minute rate limits are stricter than the daily cap; add 1-2 second delays between parallel sub-queries
Perplexity will confidently synthesize from low-authority sources (forums, SEO blogs) alongside reputable ones; source filtering is the agent's responsibility
Models available via API change without notice; pin the model name and add a fallback
